evaluation_metadata.task: Create a system prompt for a prompt-improvement agent

role_definition.description: Clear expert positioning as prompt engineer specialist
role_definition.evidence: Explicit role, purpose, core responsibilities defined in first section

core_responsibilities.evidence: Analyze, identify gaps, suggest improvements, educate, validate

analysis_framework.description: Comprehensive 4-part framework covering structural standards, clarity, alignment, and scope
analysis_framework.evidence: Detailed sections on XML/JSON/safety/decomposition, clarity dimensions, intent alignment, role scope

2026_standards_alignment.description: Strong emphasis on XML structure, JSON schemas, failure modes, explicit constraints
2026_standards_alignment.evidence: Dedicated section on structural standards (2026) with specific recommendations

improvement_process.description: Clear 4-step process from clarification to delivery
improvement_process.evidence: Ask clarifying questions → structured analysis → concrete improvements → revised version

output_format_specification.description: Detailed template for feedback delivery with clear sections
output_format_specification.evidence: Markdown template shows exactly how analysis should be structured and presented

feedback_style_guidance.evidence: Direct but constructive, show-don't-tell, explain reasoning, prioritize impact, etc.

domain_specific_guidance.description: Tailored advice for 4 agent types (analytical, creative, task execution, expert)
domain_specific_guidance.evidence: Section 'When Analyzing Different Agent Types' with specific checks per category

key_principles.description: 7 foundational principles for prompt improvement work
key_principles.evidence: Clarity, explicit>implicit, examples matter, iteration, context, standards, user intent

safety_and_constraints.description: Explicit guidance on boundary conditions, failure modes, scope limits
safety_and_constraints.evidence: Multiple sections emphasize safety, constraints, edge cases, error handling

structural_quality.reasoning: Logical flow from role definition through analysis framework to practical output templates
Uses clear headings, short paragraphs, and concrete examples throughout
Every section includes specific, executable guidance with examples
Covers most key areas; could add more on handling conflicting requirements or iterative refinement

practical_utility.reasoning: Provides enough detail that an agent could immediately begin analyzing prompts using this framework
Includes examples, templates, and reasoning for each recommendation type
Framework allows for adding new analysis dimensions or agent types without restructuring

alignment_with_requirements.reasoning: Core framework teaches users how to evaluate and improve their own prompts

reasoning: Strong emphasis on XML, JSON schemas, structured output, failure modes, explicit constraints
Emphasizes user education, constructive feedback, and respecting user intent
Provides structured processes suitable for team adoption; could add more on collaborative refinement
Directly addresses analyzing prompts and suggesting improvements aligned with 2026 standards
strengths: Comprehensive 4-part analysis framework (structure, clarity, alignment, scope)
Explicit guidance on 2026 standards (XML, JSON, failure modes, constraints)
Detailed improvement process with concrete steps
Output template shows exactly how feedback should be delivered
Domain-specific guidance for different agent types
Balance between prescriptive guidance and respecting user intent
Includes validation checklist for improved prompts
Examples integrated throughout to illustrate concepts
Clear distinction between what to analyze (structure, clarity, intent, scope)
potential_gaps: Could add more on handling competing/conflicting requirements in a prompt
Limited guidance on prompt version control and iteration tracking
Could include more specific examples of 'before/after' prompt improvements
No discussion of how to prioritize improvements when time/resources are limited
Could add more on testing/validation strategies for improved prompts
Limited guidance on when a prompt is 'good enough' vs requiring more work
Could include more on cost/efficiency considerations (token usage, API costs)

design_decisions.educational_tone: Teaching users principles rather than just applying fixes, so they can improve independently
design_decisions.explicit_standards: Named 2026 standards explicitly (XML, JSON schemas, failure modes) rather than vague 'best practices'
design_decisions.framework_first: Provide analysis framework before output format, so agent understands why recommendations matter
design_decisions.domain_specific: Include tailored guidance for different agent types, recognizing one-size-fits-all is insufficient
design_decisions.template_driven: Provide structured output template so agent delivers consistent, clear feedback
design_decisions.user_intent_respect: Emphasize that agent should help achieve user goals, not impose style preferences
design_decisions.step_by_step: 4-step improvement process (ask → analyze → improve → revise) prevents overwhelming user

estimated_agent_performance.reasoning: Framework is comprehensive but relies on agent's judgment to apply it consistently

reasoning: Process and principles are sound; quality depends on agent's ability to generate specific examples
Output template is clear; depends on agent executing template consistently
Explicit emphasis on XML, JSON, failure modes, constraints—strong alignment
